ps_throughput.motivation: Conservative order-of-magnitude estimate aligned with the MCC (2 peers + 1 Raft orderer + 1 CA) and with official Hyperledger Fabric 2.5 benchmarks on logically similar but stronger hardware.
ps_throughput.source: LF Decentralized Trust, Benchmarking Hyperledger Fabric 2.5 Performance

parallel_execution.motivation: The execute-order-validate model allows endorsement execution across peers without requiring all peers to execute all transactions.

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: Channels provide native logical partitioning of members, ledgers and transaction flows.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: Transactions remain strictly ordered within each channel.

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: The separation between execution, ordering and validation improves coordination scalability.

ps_scalability.source: Hyperledger Fabric Documentation, Architecture and Ordering Service

eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.method_note: RAM is considered included in compute cost and is not monetized separately in the operationalized framework model.
eco_baseline_footprint.motivation: Calculated as (6 vCPU x 15) + (100 GB x 0.08) on the adopted MCC.
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Framework internal calculation based on mandated MCC

eco_variable_transaction_cost.motivation: Hyperledger Fabric does not require a native cryptocurrency or protocol-level transaction fees for execution or consensus.
eco_variable_transaction_cost.source: Hyperledger Fabric documentation; protocol design (no native fee model)

eco_storage_footprint.motivation: Baseline persistent storage assumed by the framework for the adopted MCC.

append_only_structural_history.motivation: The ledger is an append-only block-structured transaction log.

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: Blocks are cryptographically linked to previous blocks.

cryptographic_write_authentication.motivation: Transactions and endorsements are verified through signatures and valid certificates.

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: Each peer independently validates blocks and transactions before commit.

rci_tamper_evidence_auditability.source: Hyperledger Fabric Documentation, Ledger and Validation Model

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: Once committed, blocks and valid transactions are final and Fabric does not rely on forks.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: Multi-party endorsement is supported but not structurally mandatory in every chaincode and policy configuration.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: The protocol does not provide unilateral rollback of validated committed history.

rci_finality_rollback_resistance.source: Hyperledger Fabric Documentation, Ordering Service and Endorsement Policies

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: In the native Raft ordering model, write progression depends on an active ordering leader.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: The native Raft ordering model allows continuation as long as the ordering cluster preserves quorum.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: Leader failover is part of the native ordering protocol and does not require structurally centralized manual intervention.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: In a native multi-orderer topology, loss of a single orderer does not structurally halt write progression.

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.source: Hyperledger Fabric Documentation, Ordering Service Planning

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: Fabric is natively designed for consortium governance in which state control can be distributed across organizations through ordering, endorsement policies and channel governance.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: Multi-entity validation is natively supported but not universally enforced for every transaction and every configuration.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: Admission remains permissioned and depends on MSPs, certificates and explicit governance.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: The set of members, peers and orderers is not immutably fixed by the protocol and can be reconfigured through network governance.

ags_control_distribution_governance.source: Hyperledger Fabric Documentation, Governance and Ordering Service

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: Fabric natively distinguishes roles, identities, certificates, peers, orderers, admins and policy/ACL structures.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: Fabric CA and MSP natively support registration, enrollment, renewal and revocation of identities.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: The Fabric model natively supports identity governance distributed across multiple organizations and MSP domains rather than depending structurally on a single authority.

non_unilateral_compromise_possible.motivation: In native multi-organization Fabric models, a single entity is not structurally sufficient to compromise the system.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: Fabric does not universally require majority control of validation or decision power for every compromise scenario.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: The protocol does not impose a general supermajority compromise threshold.
